trivia_bullets.text: Goblin Maskmaker is a red 1/2 Goblin Citizen — the Citizen subtype flags it as an unaligned Ravnican inhabitant rather than a guild-affiliated creature. In MKM's noir-mystery setting, masks and disguise are thematic pillars: the set introduced the Disguise mechanic (a named-variant of Morph granting face-down creatures ward 2) [Scryfall].

Its attack trigger reduces the cost of all face-down spells cast this turn by 1 — synergizing with every Disguise, Morph, Cloak, and Manifest card in the format. This makes it an on-curve cost-reducer for face-down strategies, enabling faster flip-up of expensive Disguise creatures like Offender at Large [Scryfall oracle text, WebSearch: MTG Ultimate Guard face-down cards article].

Flavor text: "When you can't show your own face, try one of mine!" — Speaker unnamed (the Goblin Maskmaker themselves, implied by context) [Scryfall]. The line doubles as the set's disguise thesis in miniature: identity concealment as both practical craft and survival strategy.

Artist Tomas Duchek. EDHREC rank 20543 — low uptake in Commander, consistent with a Limited-focused synergy piece rather than a Constructed staple [Scryfall]. Previewed via the official Wizards card gallery on 2024-01-18 [Scryfall preview data].

related_cards.name: Offender at Large
